channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Bitcoin $100K party: Why the price is a psychological milestone for longtime crypto believers
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are rallying on social media over their new riches after Bitcoin crossed the $100,000 threshold on Thursday. “$100,000 is a psychological milestone,” entrepreneur and investor Anthony Pompliano wrote on X.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Apex the $45M stegosaurus is on display in New York. Here's what scientists hope to learn about it
item.description: The most expensive dinosaur fossil ever discovered will be on view in New York starting this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As data centers proliferate, conflict with local communities follows
item.description: Some residents are pushing back against the world’s most powerful corporations over concerns about the economic, social and environmental health of their communities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Data distilleries: CIOs turn to new efficient enterprise data platforms
item.description: In today’s data-driven world, large enterprises are aware of the immense opportunities that data and analytics present. Yet, the true value of these initiatives is in their potential to revolutionize how data is managed and utilized across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Concerns mount over Musk’s taste for revenge
item.description: Elon Musk’s growing criticism of President-elect Trump’s opponents and industry competitors is raising concerns he may use his increasing influence to intimidate adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Burnout: A chronic epidemic in the IT industry
item.description: Burnout continues to be a widespread problem for IT organizations. It’s a complex problem that hasn’t improved since it peaked during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump's SEC Nominee Boosts Bitcoin to $100,000
item.description: Bitcoin surges past $100,000 as President-elect Trump announces Paul Atkins as SEC chair nominee, sparking market gains and speculation in cryptocurrency.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The FBI is urging telecom firms to boost network security following China’s cyberespionage
item.description: Federal authorities on Tuesday urged telecommunication companies to boost network security following a sprawling Chinese hacking campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AWS amplifies developer tools with new gen AI features
item.description: AWS is leaving no stone unturned to get generative AI tools embedded into every aspect of application development. At its annual re:Invent conference, AWS CEO Matt Garman showcased a large of number of features and tools that the company has built for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta quietly leans on rival GPT-4 despite Zuckerberg’s bold Llama claims
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has consistently championed Meta’s Llama AI model as a leader in generative AI technology, positioning it as a strong competitor to OpenAI and Google. However, behind the scenes, Meta is complementing Llama with a rival AI model to meet its internal needs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel’s CEO, Pat Gelsinger, ‘retires’ — riiiiight
item.description: Back in the 1990s, when Bill Clinton was president, people figured out they might just want to be on the internet, and Intel and Microsoft were the major technology powers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Heroku PaaS adds .NET support
item.description: Meeting a longstanding request from users, Salesforce’s Heroku — one of the original platforms as a service (PaaS) — has unveiled official support for .NET development. Heroku also made its Heroku Next Generation Platform available in a pilot.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI
item.description: There is a need to create secure computing capabilities that maintain the privacy and confidentiality of AI models and data sets. The post Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: If you want security, start with secure products
item.description: While in these post-CrowdStrike days it is reasonable to think one of the best ways to improve endpoint security in any business is to replace Windows with Apple devices, that’s just the starting point in a serious security journey. But it’s a pretty good starting point.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Army Guard to prioritize application visibility across states and territories in 2025
item.description: Fiscal 2025 is shaping up to be busy for the Army National Guard as it pushes to complete migration of all end-user devices to Entra ID and Intune. The post Army Guard to prioritize application visibility across states and territories in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AT&T sees earnings growth over next 3 years; eyes more than $40B in anticipated shareholder returns
item.description: AT&T anticipates earnings growth over the next three years thanks to the momentum of 5G and fiber services.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robotic shorts give your legs a boost to walk farther and tire less
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how WalkON enhances walking and jogging with sensors and artificial tendons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AWS Database Migration Service gets gen AI-powered schema conversion
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has updated its Database Migration Service’s schema conversion feature with a new generative AI-powered capability that automates schema conversion to a great extent.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AWS brings RAG evaluation and LLM-as-a-judge feature to Amazon Bedrock
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has updated Amazon Bedrock with features designed to help enterprises streamline the testing of applications before deployment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4
